concerns.text: Task T020 ('Implement the Baseline MAML runner') is listed as a prerequisite for T021 ('orchestrate 5 independent training runs'). However, T020 only mentions a 'config flag' and does not explicitly include the execution of the 5-seed baseline runs required to generate the data for the Hotelling's T-squared test in T022. The consumer (T022) requires the artifact (baseline results) which is not fully produced by T020 as described. T021 must explicitly include the execution of the baseline runs, or T020 must be split to include the run execution.

Task T022 ('Implement the statistical analysis script... Hotelling's T-squared') is listed before T024 ('Add handling for baseline failure cases'). The statistical analysis (T022) requires the logic to handle covariance singularity (a baseline failure case) to avoid crashing. T024 describes the handling but is placed after the implementation of the test itself. The error handling logic (T024) must be integrated into or precede the statistical test implementation (T022) to ensure the consumer (T022) does not fail on edge cases.

Task T012 ('Implement the differentiable Calcium-Wave ODE') and T013 ('Implement the MAML inner-loop update rule') are marked [P] (parallel). However, T013 explicitly depends on the output of T012 (the $h_t$ factor) to perform the modulation. While they are in different files, the semantic dependency (producer T012 -> consumer T013) means they cannot be truly parallel in a single-threaded execution flow without a mock/stub. The [P] tag is misleading here; they should be sequential or T013 should be marked as dependent on T012 completion.
